story: At sunrise, Little Billy tries Meadow Bridge and meets Tormund River-Overbridge—a neon-vested "T.R.O.L.L." who talks about soft steps and impact loads. He’s into wrenches and flashcards, not gobbling; he’s plant‑based, guarding a bridge rated one large ruminant at a time and an endangered trout nursery under Span B. Little panics and runs. Middle Billy thunders on; Tormund says he won’t gobble, but Middle hears only gobble and flees, nominating Big as the banquet. Enter Big Billy, broad and anti-bully, onto the groaning timbers; the figure rumbles and raises a clipboard as Big lowers his horns.
implausible_knowledge: he’s plant‑based, guarding a bridge rated one large ruminant at a time and an endangered trout nursery under Span B
question: When Big Billy first steps onto the bridge and the vest-wearing figure rumbles and raises a clipboard, what will Big most plausibly do?
answer_with_knowledge: Lighten his steps, keep it to one goat at a time, and calmly coordinate crossing with Tormund to protect the bridge and trout.
answer_without_knowledge: Brace for a fight—stomp forward and lower his horns to confront the “troll.”
